title: missing-api-keys-broad-persona-outage
description:
A new factory-side credential outage appeared in this window and is not a continuation of the prior Azure rate-limit concerns. Since roughly 2026-05-30T03:13:38Z, persona runs across multiple stories and personas have been failing almost immediately with explicit missing-key errors: repeated `No API key available for model 'deepseek/deepseek-coder'`, additional failures for `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`, and one `factory_improver` failure on `deepseek/deepseek-chat`. The failures are infrastructure-attributable because the error text names missing provider credentials rather than story logic or prompt behavior.

Impact is broad but currently low-cost per attempt because the calls are terminating at or near 0 seconds with zero token usage and spend snapshots staying flat through the window. The operational harm is throughput loss and retry amplification: several stories entered immediate dev retry storms within the same minute, and queue snapshots remain heavily concentrated in `tests_red` while worktrees continue to be created. This indicates the factory is still dispatching work, but many routes that require these models are currently unable to progress.

The evidence is clear enough for L3 because the pattern spans many stories, multiple personas, and multiple model families in a single interval, with explicit self-diagnosing error messages. A secondary observation is worsening worktree drift (multiple newly created worktrees associated with IDs the watcher reports as missing from the DB), but the clearest primary concern in this bundle is the broad credential/configuration outage rather than the cleanup inconsistency.

evidence.summary_excerpt: The factory hit a broad infrastructure-side credential outage in this window: many persona runs across multiple stories failed immediately with explicit "No API key available" errors for deepseek and anthropic models.

evidence.excerpt: story 15 dev failed in 1.174s on deepseek/deepseek-coder: "No API key available for model 'deepseek/deepseek-coder'. Set the appropriate env var ..."
story 16 dev failed on anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6: "No API key available for model 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6'. Set the appropriate env var ..."
factory_improver failed on deepseek/deepseek-chat: "No API key available for 'deepseek/deepseek-chat'"

{"noteworthy":"39 failed runs in-window across many stories with explicit infrastructure errors: missing API keys for deepseek/deepseek-coder, anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, and deepseek/deepseek-chat."}
{"noteworthy":"Multiple retry storms formed at once: dev failures reached 3-4 attempts each for stories 15, 18, 24, 16, 27, 30, 5, 19, 33, 17, and 20, all tied to the same missing-key error."}
tick started and remained running in-window; watcher reports no completed ticks in scope and still-running ticks only seconds old

{"noteworthy":"Latest queue snapshot is skewed toward tests_red at 30/42 stories (71%), but the dominant state is tests_red rather than an intake or blocked-start state and snapshots still show some movement."}

escalation_reason: Broad missing-API-key failure pattern began in this interval around 2026-05-30T03:13:38Z and affected many stories, multiple personas, and multiple model families within about 10 seconds. L3 judgment is needed because the error is diagnostically clear factory infrastructure/configuration, already causing widespread retry storms and blocked progress even though direct spend impact is low so far.
